eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)

never: adverb. At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.
nor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
onto: preposition. Arriving upon or on top of (speaking of a physical or metaphorical movement).

rente: noun. In France, interest payable by government on indebtedness; the bonds, shares, stocks, etc. that represent government indebtedness.
ret: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Retta.
rete: noun. A network of blood vessels or nerves.
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
revet: verb. To face (an embankment, etc.) with masonry, wood, or other material.
revote: noun. An act of voting again
roe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ronji.

rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rote: noun. Mechanical routine; a fixed, habitual, repetitive, or mechanical course of procedure.
tee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huehuetla Tepehua.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tenor: noun. A musical range or section higher than bass and lower than alto.
tern: noun. Any of various seabirds of the subfamily Sterninae (of the family Laridae) that are similar to gulls but are smaller and have a
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
toner: noun. Powder used in laser printers and photocopiers to form the text and images on the printed paper.
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
toon: noun. A cartoon, especially an animated television show.
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
tree: noun. A perennial woody plant taller and larger than a shrub with a wooden trunk and, at some distance from the ground, having leaves and
trone: noun. A type of steelyard (weighing machine) for heavy wares, such as wool, consisting of two horizontal bars crossing each other, beaked
trove: noun. A treasure trove; a collection of treasure.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
vent: noun. An opening through which gases, especially air, can pass.
vert: noun. A green colour, now only in heraldry; represented in engraving by diagonal parallel lines 45 degrees counter-clockwise.
vet: noun. A veterinarian or veterinary surgeon.
veto: noun. A political right to disapprove of (and thereby stop) the process of a decision, a law etc.
vetoer: noun. One who exercises the power to refuse approval.
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
vote: noun. A formalized choice on legally relevant measures such as employment or appointment to office or a proceeding about a legal dispute.
